channel.title: CNBC International TV
channel.description:
CNBC is the recognized world leader in business news and provides real-time financial market coverage and business information to approximately 385 million homes worldwide.
With international headquarters in London and Singapore, CNBC International covers news and features from Europe, the Middle East, Africa and Asia Pacific.
Subscribe to CNBC International TV for the very best of CNBC's international programming, including top business news moments and award-winning original documentaries and feature series.
For real-time news updates from around the world, visit CNBC's website: http://www.cnbc.com
Subscribe to our other YouTube channel here: https://www.youtube.com/user/CNBCInternational/
Like our Facebook page www.facebook.com/cnbcinternational
Follow us on Twitter https://twitter.com/CNBCi

item.description: Saudi Minister of State for Foreign Affairs Adel Al-Jubeir says the kingdom's rulers are serious about human rights. Mistakes happen, he said acknowledging the murder of Saudi journalist Jamal Khashoggi in Turkey.
item.title: 'We're very serious about human rights': Saudi minister on rights and the murder of Jamal Khashoggi
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Antonio Tajani, national coordinator of Italy's Forza Italia party and member of European Parliament, discusses the 5-Star Movement's refusal to back Prime Minister Mario Draghi's national unity government in a parliamentary vote.
item.title: It's impossible to work with the 5-Star Movement, says Forza Italia's Tajani
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: President Biden is scheduled to touch down in Saudi Arabia later today, CNBC's Hadley Gamble reports from Jeddah
item.title: President Biden is scheduled to touch down in Saudi Arabia later today
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Adel al-Jubeir says Saudi Arabia's policy is to engage with different countries, including both the U.S. and China which are the kingdom's major partners. We build bridges with people, we don't see one as exclusive of the other, the Saudi climate envoy told CNBC's Hadley Gamble.
item.title: The U.S. and China are both important partners to Saudi Arabia, says Saudi's al-Jubeir
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Viktor Shvets of Macquarie Capital says that's because inflation isn't arising from excess demand but from disruptions to the goods and services market as well as the labor market.
item.title: Monetary policy can't really address most of current inflationary pressures: Macquarie Capital
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Rebeca Minguela, founder and CEO of Clarity AI, says that's a challenge that applies not only to the climate crisis, but also to areas like diversity and other social issues.
item.title: Transparency is a 'huge problem' when it comes to sustainable investment, says Clarity AI CEO
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Qian Wang of Vanguard says some central banks in Asia's emerging markets are accelerating monetary policy tightening partly because of currency depreciation.
item.title: Currency depreciation is happening across Asia, says economist
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Michael Harris, founder of Cribstone Strategic Macro, discusses the prospect of the G-7 imposing a price cap on Russian oil exports.
item.title: Strategist says proposed price cap on Russian oil exports likely to benefit India and Turkey
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Dan Yergin of S&P Global discusses how inflation and the risk of a recession are affecting oil prices.
item.title: Dan Yergin explains why crude prices are falling
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Sweden Prime Minister Magdalena Andersson tells CNBC that Europe is going through a difficult economic situation. Meanwhile, Latvia's Prime Minister Arturs Karins says Ukrainians are paying with their lives and Europe with its wallet.
item.title: 'We're definitely in a difficult economic situation,' Sweden PM says
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Jim Townsend of the CNAS Transatlantic Security Program, who served as deputy assistant secretary of defense for Europe and NATO under the Obama administration, says Europe's view on China is more circumspect than what the U.S. might want it to be.
item.title: NATO sees Russia as a 'direct threat,' but not China, says think tank
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Reeve Collins, CEO of BlockV and a co-founder of Tether, explains the difference between algorithmic stablecoins and those that are backed by reserves and provides his outlook for crypto regulation.
item.title: Missteps and failures in crypto are inevitable, Tether co-founder says
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Constantinos Herodotou, Governor of the Central Bank of Cyprus and Member of the ECB Governing Council, says inflation is likley to peak in the euro zone this year and shares his views on how the ECB should adapt its interest rate policy.
item.title: Bank of Cyprus governor says gradualism warranted as ECB normalizes monetary policy
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Sri Mulyani Indrawati, Indonesia's finance minister, discusses U.S. Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen's proposal of a price cap on Russian oil.
item.title: A price cap on Russian oil won't solve the problem of supply, says Indonesia's finance minister
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: C. Raja Mohan of the Institute of South Asian Studies discusses Sri Lanka's economic crisis. He says there has to be some understanding between the international creditors and the international institutions to support [the country's] path to recovery.
item.title: Sri Lanka crisis: There has to be understanding between international creditors, says researcher
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: EU Commissioner for Economic Affairs Paolo Gentiloni talks to CNBC about Italy's latest political uncertainty and the country's economy.
item.title: EU very much likes to have Draghi leading Italy, official says amid new political uncertainty
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Former U.S. Energy Secretary Dan Brouillette joins CNBC’s Capital Connection to discuss President Biden's trip to Saudi Arabia.
item.title: Biden administration needs to treat Saudi Arabia 'with respect,' says former U.S. energy secretary
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Creon Butler of Chatham House says China is unlikely to continue with its current level of spending as the country has been burnt in a number of ways by its debt issues.
item.title: China may not continue spending a large amount of money on its Belt and Road Initiative: Think tank
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Trevor Greetham, head of multi-asset at Royal London Asset Management, discusses historical bear markets and says there could be a long, grinding period ahead for stock markets.
item.title: 'Don't get too sucked back in': Fund manager discusses what past bear markets can teach us
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Christian Mueller-Glissmann, head of asset allocation research at Goldman Sachs, said high-growth tech companies are more vulnerable in the current macroeconomic environment.
item.title: We're still above the trough in tech, Goldman Sachs says
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: David Loevinger of the TCW Group says the uncertainty will linger as long as the country's zero-Covid policy remains.
item.title: China's economic recovery is going to be 'very slow,' says asset management firm
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Crypto hedge fund Three Arrows Capital has fallen into liquidation. The company is yet another victim of the plunge in cryptocurrency prices which has exposed its highly leveraged bets. CNBC's Arjun Kharpal reports on how 3AC got here.
item.title: Crypto hedge fund Three Arrows Capital plunges into liquidation. This is how it happened
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: China's e-sports revenue totaled $403.1 million in 2021, making it the largest single market in the world, according to Niko Partners. Alexander Champlin, head of e-sports at Niko Partners, discusses whether China's stricter gaming regulation could affect the industry.
item.title: China remains world's largest e-sport market despite gaming crackdown, says Niko Partners
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Saudi Arabia's climate envoy Adel al-Jubeir says his country is committed to ensuring that markets are adequately supplied with crude oil, and will consult with OPEC+ on whether there's a need to bring more oil on to the markets.
item.title: Saudi Arabia is committed to stabilizing international oil markets, its climate envoy says
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: S&P Global’s Dan Yergin joins CNBC's Capital Connection to discuss President Joe Biden's trip to Saudi Arabia.
item.title: Dan Yergin on Biden's trip to Saudi Arabia: 'Oil is on his mind'
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Cryptocurrency prices have crashed in the last few months as the industry enters a new crypto winter. Crypto has seen this before, most recently in 2018. But things are different this time. CNBC's Arjun Kharpal reports on the factors that have driven the price plunge in the most recent crypto cycle.
item.title: This 'crypto winter' is different from the last. Here's why
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Speaking ahead of the Group of 20 finance ministers' meeting in Bali, U.S. Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen says the Department of the Treasury is taking its own steps to cool inflation in the short term, while being supportive of the Federal Reserve's efforts.
item.title: Inflation is unacceptably high and bringing it down should be the top priority, says Janet Yellen
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Constantinos Herodotou, Governor of the Central Bank of Cyprus and Member of the ECB Governing Council, speaks to CNBC's Annette Weisbach about negotiations for a euro zone fragmentation tool, and lays out his inflation predictions.
item.title: ECB's Herodotou sees euro zone inflation peaking this year
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Shanta Devarajan of Georgetown University discusses Sri Lanka’s debt crisis.
item.title: Sri Lanka is on the verge of becoming a 'fragile state,' says professor
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Christopher Smart of Barings says that higher costs can squeeze margins and dampen outlook, but consumer spending and job markets still indicate a strong economy and moderate earnings growth.
item.title: Barings: Margins, not topline results, are the focus for this earnings season
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Dan Brouillette, who served as the U.S. secretary of energy from 2019 to 2021, says he hopes U.S. President Joe Biden will, during his visit to Saudi Arabia, raise questions that go beyond oil production, such as how the country's hydrogen market can be developed.
item.title: Saudi Arabia is going to be a major player in hydrogen, says former U.S. energy secretary
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Marc Ginsberg, former U.S. Ambassador to Morocco, discusses U.S. President Joe Biden's trip to Israel and what he thinks Israelis want the most from his visit.
item.title: Israel wants Biden to reaffirm his commitment to the country's security, says ex-U.S. diplomat
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Michael Howell, managing director at CrossBorder Capital, discusses the outlook for the euro/dollar currency pair and his views on so-called safe-haven assets as recession risks intensify.
item.title: Strategist says investors are quickly running out of safe assets as recession risks grow
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Gavin Chia of the wealth management platform discusses its expansion plans and how it educates clients about investments.
item.title: Futu Singapore says it is looking to expand in Southeast Asia
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Saliya Pieris of the Bar Association of Sri Lanka says this is the third time a state of emergency has been declared.
item.title: Remains to be seen when Sri Lanka's state of emergency will be lifted, says bar association
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Jeffrey Schott of the Peterson Institute for International Economics says Europe is in a very tight situation and is playing for time.
item.title: G-7 has to prepare for a gas shutdown as energy crisis continues, says think tank
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Tanah Sullivan of the company says there are serious blood, sweat and tears that have gone into helping it achieve its goals by 2030.
item.title: Indonesia's GoTo discusses what it needs to achieve its sustainability goals
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Marc Ostwald of ADM Investor Services International discusses the challenge that the ECB faces in balancing two very conflicting interests.
item.title: The European Central Bank started hiking rates too late, says futures brokerage company
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Tan See Leng, Singapore's manpower minister, says the country's labor market continues to be tight but its long-term unemployment has dropped.
item.title: Long-term unemployment in Singapore is close to pre-Covid levels, says minister
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Tina Fordham, founder of Fordham Global Foresight, discusses the overturning of Roe v. Wade and says there is disbelief on the part of U.S. allies over the reversal of what's seen as a fundamental right.
item.title: Reversal of Roe v. Wade is a 'business risk' for the United States, says geopolitical strategist
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Asanga Abeyagoonasekera of the Millennium Project says there's been a thorough breach of the social contract between the public and its rulers, adding that the trust deficit is widening.
item.title: Protests in Sri Lanka are moving to a different stage, says think tank
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Ole Hansen, head of commodity strategy at Saxo Bank, weighs in on U.S. President Biden's gas tax plans, his outlook for oil prices, and what the commodities sector can tell us about a potential recession.
item.title: Commodities rout pointing to recession fears, says analyst
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: John Goddard, vice chair of sustainability at L.E.K Consulting, discusses the results of its survey, which he says has yielded insights on how corporations are embedding sustainability into the business.
item.title: Nearly 70% of respondents say sustainability will be an important growth factor, survey finds
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Dan Yergin of S&P Global says Iran, Russia and China are also on U.S. President Joe Biden's agenda for his visit.
item.title: Question of spare oil capacity will be on Biden's mind during his Middle East trip, says Dan Yergin
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Brian Tan of the bank says Singapore's economy is definitely operating above its potential.
item.title: Singapore's inflation will likely be 'quite sustained' in the second half of 2022, says Barclays
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Ben van Beurden, CEO of the energy company, says an ever tighter oil and gas market is appearing.
item.title: Shell CEO says OPEC probably doesn't have as much spare capacity of oil as many people think
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Rachel Barton of Accenture discusses the results of an Accenture/S&P survey.
item.title: Accenture strategist names 3 factors that are damaging business confidence
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Ahmed Saeed of the regional development bank says there's always a risk, as U.S. interest rates rise, that when it sneezes, the world catches a cold, but Southeast Asian economies have been taking their vitamins. He was speaking on the sidelines of the Group of 20 meeting of financial leaders in Bali.
item.title: Southeast Asia looks 'pretty good' in the face of rising interest rates, says Asian Development Bank
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Brian McNamara discusses the London Stock Exchange listing of GSK spin-off Haleon, a consumer health business.
item.title: Market will decide valuation of company, says Haleon CEO
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Mohamad Al-Ississ, finance minister of Jordan, speaks exclusively to CNBC's Hadley Gamble.
item.title: Jordan finance minister: Jordan will be resilient to economic shocks
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Luke Hickmore, investment director at Abrdn, explains why a recession is his base case, and adds that it won't be a full-blown one but a consumer recession.
item.title: The recession coming won't be a 'normal' one, but it's coming, strategist says
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Jordan's King Abdullah II says he would be one of the first people that would endorse a Middle East NATO, but the vision of such an alliance must be very clear.
item.title: Jordan's king says he believes a Middle Eastern 'NATO' can be built with like-minded countries
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Chris Carey of Wells Fargo Securities says that while Pepsico can navigate and price consumer staples to their respective markets, strengthening of the U.S. dollar may affect multinational earnings, and increased retailer sensitivity to higher pricing requires close monitoring.
item.title: Retail pricing environment will evolve over the next few quarters: Wells Fargo
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Shares in UAE telco e& rose more than 5% on Monday after the company formerly known as Etisalat announced a major rebrand and strategic restructure.
item.title: Our aspiration is to accelerate growth, says e& CEO
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description:
Adel Al-Jubeir, Saudi Arabia’s Minister of State for Foreign Affairs and Envoy for Climate Affairs speaks to CNBC's Hadley Gamble in Jeddah about Saudi Arabia’s climate policy, oil production and President Biden's visit to the Kingdom.
_________________________________________________

item.title: Watch Saudi Arabia’s Climate Envoy Adel Al-Jubeir Full Interview
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Neil Atkinson, independent oil analyst, discusses diplomatic relations between the U.S. and Saudi Arabia and how this is likely to influence energy markets.
item.title: Biden's visit to Saudi Arabia unlikely to result in a triumph for either party, oil analyst says
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Richard Cho of Hanwha Defense says supply chain disruptions and rising costs have been hampering the company's activities in many ways.
item.title: South Korean defense firm says meeting demand has been difficult because of rising costs
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description:
Prince Turki Al-Faisal, Saudi Arabia’s former intelligence chief and former Ambassador to the United States speaks to CNBC's Hadley Gamble in Jeddah ahead of President Biden's visit to the Kingdom.
_______________________
Subscribe to CNBC International TV: http://cnb.cx/2wAkfMv

Follow us on Twitter https://twitter.com/cnbci
item.title: Full Interview: Prince Turki Al-Faisal on President Biden's Trip to the Kingdom
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: CNBC's Silvia Amaro speaks to diplomats and policymakers at the European Council in Brussels, as fears grow about the region's future gas supplies from Russia.
item.title: Europe is growing increasingly worried about a total shutdown of Russian gas
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Giovanni Orsina, professor of contemporary history and director of the School of Government at Luiss-Guido Carli University, discusses the Italian president's refusal to accept Prime Minister Mario Draghi's resignation.
item.title: Possibility of Italian election in the fall should not be ruled out, says professor
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Mathias Cormann, secretary-general of the Organization for Economic Cooperation and Development, responds to a question about U.S. Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen's proposal of a price cap on Russian oil.
item.title: 'There are alternatives to Russian oil,' OECD chief says of U.S. call for a Russian oil price cap
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Prince Turki Al-Faisal, Saudi Arabia’s former intelligence chief and former ambassador to the United States, says President Joe Biden is coming to Saudi Arabia as “a much diminished president.”
item.title: Prince Turki: President Biden is coming to Saudi as 'a much diminished president'
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Stephen Roach of Yale University says China's weak recovery makes it vulnerable to another shock, such as more Covid lockdowns and any one of a number of possibilities that we can't even imagine right now.
item.title: Stephen Roach says China's economy is set for a weak rebound
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Alexander Treves of JPMorgan Asset Management discusses why that's the case even though the company is underweight on China A-shares.
item.title: JPMorgan explains why it likes Chinese tech stocks
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Tan Teck Leng of UBS Global Wealth Management explains why it's a little bit worried about near-term sentiment.
item.title: UBS discusses the outlook for the dollar-yuan
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Cleveland Fed President Loretta Mester tells CNBC's Annette Weisbach about the likely outcome of the Federal Reserve's July meeting and gives her outlook on inflation and its impacts on the economy.
item.title: Fed's Mester says market volatility is probably here to stay
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Ben Gutteridge, director of model portfolio services at Invesco, says the stock market reaction has been relatively benign so far.
item.title: Stock markets 'didn't puke' on the latest U.S. inflation print, investor says
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: President of Kosovo Vjosa Osmani-Sadriu tells CNBC's Silvia Amaro why her country is pushing to join the EU, and how she perceives the bloc has dealt with the invasion of Ukraine.
item.title: President of Kosovo: We deserve to be in EU
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Vishnu Varathan of the bank says the Monetary Authority of Singapore has done a lot more [monetary policy] tightening upfront, giving it much more flexibility.
item.title: Inflation risks in Singapore will 'certainly be tackled,' says Mizuho Bank
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Ganeshan Wignaraja of ODI Global discusses what President Gotabaya Rajapaksa's resignation might mean for the country's future.
item.title: Sri Lanka: Political maneuvering behind the scenes is the 'fly in the ointment,' says think tank
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: A root cause of inflation is too many people chasing too few goods, and those people now are millions of millennials who are finally purchasing assets, says Bill Smead, CIO at Smead Capital Management.
item.title: No one is talking about millennials' role in high inflation, investor says
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: However, a spokesperson for the National Bureau of Statistics of China said the country is performing better than the U.S. and Europe when it comes to inflation. CNBC's Evelyn Cheng reports.
item.title: China faces many challenges in achieving its full-year economic targets, says statistics bureau
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: European Commissioner Paolo Gentiloni comments on the prospects of an oil price cap as suggested by the United States.
item.title: EU not yet ready to propose an oil price cut, official says
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Bryan Donaghey, head of business at Emperador and CEO of Emperador-owned Whyte & Mackay, discusses its secondary listing on the Singapore Exchange and its international expansion plans.
item.title: Philippine liquor company says its expansion plans are not limited to Asia-Pacific
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Jirayut Topp Srupsrisopa, CEO of Bitkub, says there's usually a golden year for the industry every four years, but we have already passed the golden year in 2021.
item.title: Thai crypto exchange urges caution on investing in the market
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Alexander Irving, European transport analyst at AB Bernstein, discusses which airlines are best suited to weather the travel chaos and staff shortages plaguing a number of large airports across the region.
item.title: Bernstein analyst likes these two airlines despite travel chaos
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: William Roebuck, executive vice president at the Arab Gulf States Institute in Washington, formerly U.S. ambassador to Bahrain, discusses the criticism surrounding President Joe Biden's trip to the Middle East.
item.title: Biden's visit to Saudi Arabia was a difficult one, says former U.S. diplomat
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Saudi Minister of State for Foreign Affairs Adel al-Jubeir says the integration of China is important to the stability of the global economic system, and everybody loses when one engages in beggar-thy-neighbor policies that could adversely impact other countries.
item.title: Any conflict with China is disruptive to the world, says Saudi minister Adel al-Jubeir
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Adel al-Jubeir, Saudi Arabia's minister of state for foreign affairs, says things could spin out of control if there's an escalation in the Russia-Ukraine war — as can already be seen in disruptions in energy markets, refugee crisis and food supply.
item.title: Escalation of war in Ukraine means 'we all pay the price,' says Saudi minister al-Jubeir
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Fatih Birol, the executive director of the International Energy Agency, discusses clean energy, the climate crisis and price caps.
item.title: Emissions 'don't have a passport': IEA's Birol talks clean energy, climate change and price caps
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Albert Park of the bank says although the reliance on rice as opposed to wheat or corn in Asia has managed to keep inflation in check, he discusses the factors that could lead to the increase in rice prices.
item.title: Asian central banks are being vigilant on inflation, ADB says
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Yves Bonzon, group CIO at Julius Baer, discusses expectations for the second-quarter earnings season and liquidity in the market.
item.title: Clues on profitability will be ‘absolutely crucial’ this earnings season, Julius Baer CIO says
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description:

item.title: Full Interview: Presiden Indonesia Joko Widodo mengenai Warisan, G20, Elon Musk dan lain-lain
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Clemens Fuest, president of the IFO Institute, talks to CNBC's Julianna Tatelbaum.
item.title: IFO: Europe energy will face chllenges in winter when heating is needed
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Florian Ielpo, head of Macro and Multi-Asset Portfolio Manager at Lombard Odier Investment, discusses the rise in risk aversion in global markets.
item.title: Bad news is good news for markets right now, portfolio manager says
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: In conversation with CNBC's Dan Murphy, Thomas Flohr, the founder and CEO of private jet subscription service VistaJet, discussed his company's carbon neutral commitment and why he doesn't think we'll see the electrification of the aviation industry anytime soon.
item.title: VistaJet chief: Electric planes are decades away
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Trita Parsi of The Quincy Institute says that when it comes to reasserting American leadership, there's likely to be more rhetoric than substance.
item.title: Biden's Middle East trip is 'quite questionable,' says think tank
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: VistaJet founder & CEO, Thomas Flohr welcomes CNBC's Dan Murphy on board one of the airline's latest Bombardier Global 7500 planes, explains how he disrupted a sector and shares his advice for getting ahead in business.
item.title: On board a Bombardier Global 7500 private jet
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Prince Turki Al-Faisal, Saudi Arabia’s former intelligence chief and former ambassador to the United States, tells CNBC that America needs to engage with the kingdom in order to stabilize oil prices, ahead of President Biden's trip to Saudi Arabia.
item.title: Prince Turki: America needs to engage with the kingdom in order to stabilize oil prices
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Renee McGowan of Mercer discusses the results of its Cost of Living survey.
item.title: Housing in many Asian cities is expensive regardless of what the inflation rate is, says consultancy
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Charlie Robertson, author of The Time-Travelling Economist and global chief economist at Renaissance Capital, discusses the outlook for emerging markets.
item.title: I've put some of my own money into emerging markets, says economist
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Daniel Boardman-Weston, CEO & CIO at BRI Wealth Management, says that tech stocks — hard-hit in the recent market sell-off — are poised to stabilize over the coming months, with top-performing companies proving resilient.
item.title: The stage is set for more stability in tech stocks, says investor
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Speaking ahead of the Group of 20 finance ministers' meeting in Bali, U.S. Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen says a price cap on Russian oil would be one of our most powerful tools to address the pain that Americans and families across the world are feeling at the gas pump and the grocery store right now.
item.title: A price cap on Russian oil would deny Putin the revenue his war machine needs, says Janet Yellen
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Ida Wolden Bache, governor at the Norges Bank, speaks to CNBC's Julianna Tatelbaum.
item.title: Norges Bank governor discusses Norway's biggest rate hike in 20 years
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Shrikant Chouhan of Kotak Securities says the multinational conglomerate is very aggressive in its digitalization efforts.
item.title: India's Reliance Industries is going to do well, says stock broking firm
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description:
When it comes to finding a career, the generally accepted advice is to look for something you enjoy and are good at.
However, Alex Liu, managing partner and chairman at management consulting firm Kearney, took it one step further.
“There’s this ancient Japanese concept called ‘ikigai,’ where you’re trying to balance what you’re passionate about, versus what you can get paid for, and what the world needs and what you’re good at,” Liu told CNBC “Make It.”
Liu said that it’s very easy to focus too heavily on just one or two of these factors, which could prevent you from finding the right work for you.
However, he believes this principle helped him to not only find his career but also his calling.
“I’m in a profession where I’m coaching companies and teams to reach their full potential. So, in some sense, I found my own ikigai by helping other people reach their full potential.”
This is just one of five of Alex Liu’s biggest lessons. Watch the video above for more.
---
Subscribe to CNBC International TV: http://cnb.cx/2wAkfMv
Subscribe to CNBC International: http://cnb.cx/2gft82z
Like our Facebook page https://www.facebook.com/cnbcinternational
Follow us on Instagram https://www.instagram.com/cnbcinternational/
Follow us on Twitter https://twitter.com/cnbci
item.title: How tapping into this ancient Japanese concept could help you figure out your calling
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Rudiger von Fritsch of Berlin Global Advisors, formerly German ambassador to Russia, discusses the European Union's decision to grant Ukraine and Moldova candidate status.
item.title: Putin's ambitions go beyond occupying Ukrainian territory, says former German ambassador
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Michael Yoshikami, founder and CEO of Destination Wealth Management, says there's lots of uncertainty over what the U.S. Federal Reserve is going to do.
item.title: If we're not already in a recession we're about to be, says wealth management firm
item.author: CNBC International TV

item.description: Marc-Alexander Christ, CEO and co-founder of SumUp, discusses the payment company's latest funding round and falling valuations in fintech.
item.title: Fintech is seeing 'healthy' pullback after an 'exuberant' 2 years, SumUp CEO says
item.author: CNBC International TV
